ocr.text: The data presented in the accompanying table illustrates the monthly fluctuations in maximum values recorded from January
through June. This period marks a critical phase for assessing performance metrics, particularly in the context of operational
efficiency and output levels. Notably, the maximum recorded values exhibit a discernible upward trend, culminating in an
impressive peak of 8.30 in June. This increase is indicative of enhanced productivity and possibly improved resource allocation

strategies implemented during the first half of the year.

Furthermore, the values for January and February, which stand at 5.10
and 5.20 respectively, suggest a relatively stable environment early in the year. However, the subsequent rise in maximum
values during March and April, reaching 5.80 and escalating to 8.30 by June, reflects a significant shift in performance
dynamics.
Jan.  Feb.  Mar.  Apr.  May  June
Standard deviation  2.48  2.39  1.85  1.36  1.20  1.07
Variation coefficient  2.99  4.44  0.84  0.24  0.11  0.07
July  Aug.  Sep.  Oct.  Nov.  Dec.
Standard deviation  1.59  1.45  1.20  1.33  1.27  1.88
Variation coefficient  0.09  0.09  0.09  0.13  0.22  0.76
Such data is essential for stakeholders aiming to understand seasonal trends and the effectiveness of implemented initiatives. By
analyzing these maximum values, organizations can better position themselves for future growth opportunities while also
addressing any underlying challenges that may arise in subsequent months. The trends observed in this data provide a
foundation for strategic planning and informed decision-making as the year progresses.
